label: Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning

node.label: Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
node.definition: Knowledge-graph systems organize entities and relations for semantic search, integration, multi-hop retrieval, probabilistic inference, and grounded generation.
node.why_it_matters: They preserve explicit structure and lineage that can be difficult to recover from embeddings or parameters alone.
limitations: Ontology design, entity resolution, freshness, relation confidence, and graph construction cost dominate quality.

dependencies.rationale: Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning integrates Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning integrates Graph RAG & structured retrieval as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning integrates Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
In this atlas graph, Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning has an `applies` dependency on Graph transformers with `common` strength.
Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning integrates Provenance, citations & evidence tracking as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning integrates Expert systems, production rules & rule engines as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
In this atlas graph, Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning has an `applies` dependency on Probabilistic & differentiable programming with `optional` strength.
